prompt: The generic array creation causes a compile error. What is the bug?
choices: Arrays cannot be returned from generic methods
Java prohibits creating generic type arrays (new T[size]) because type parameters are erased at runtime; the JVM doesn't know what type to create
The size parameter must be declared as long for array creation
Generic arrays must be created with null elements first
explanation: Due to type erasure, T is not known at runtime, so new T[size] cannot be compiled. Fix options: (1) pass a Class<T> and use Array.newInstance(cls, size); (2) create Object[] and cast: return (T[]) new Object[size]; (with @SuppressWarnings). This is why ArrayList uses Object[] internally.
